Jeremy Lowery.
Experiential Architecture.

A place-based brand ecosystem. Nature tells the story, story becomes the experience, and experience becomes a world the right people find their way into.

March 2026  ·  St. Augustine, FL
Read on
01 The Full Ecosystem

Everything we discussed
has a through-line.

Hidden things made discoverable. Land as a living business model. A curated world the right creatives are initiated into. Not three separate ideas — one organism with multiple facets.

I

Hidden History in Plain Sight

The fountain. The magnolias. The urn nobody knows they're standing next to. Physical spaces that already hold stories — surfaced, made immersive, made magnetic. QR codes. Garden objects with lore. Guerrilla storytelling rooted in place.

II

Land as a Living Business Model

Curated experiences on land — gardens, retreats, events, discovery trails — that generate multiple revenue streams. The land stays stewarded. We architect the stories that connect to it.

III

The Creative Retreat Engine

Creatives with resources — working on an album, a novel, a TED talk — matched with the right off-grid location, facilitators, and land. Small groups. High-touch. Screen-free. Creatives leave with a plan and a renewed sense of direction.

IV

A Mycelial Network

Not a public brand. An underground, speakeasy model — the discovery process is the filter, referrals are the door. The right people find their way in because someone they trust sent them. The exclusivity of this project is the product. This has celebrity potential.

02 What Gets Built

Five deliverables.
Six months.

This is one sequenced intentionally — you can't design an offer before you know who it's for, and you can't pitch investors before the brand exists. The strategy is to create the order, first.

01
Month 1
$2,500–$5,000
Market value

ICP + Deep Target Market Research

Who has the problem, what they're already paying for, and what they're desperate for that doesn't exist yet. Your starving crowd — mapped, profiled, and validated. Demographic, psychographic, behavioral. This becomes the foundation everything else is built on.

02
Month 2
$3,000–$8,000
Market value

Offer + Business Model Design

Dream outcome defined. Value stacked. Retreat tiers structured — solo residency, small group, membership access. Pricing logic built on what the outcome is worth, not what feels comfortable to charge. Revenue model mapped across all expressions of the ecosystem.

03
Month 3
$8,000–$20,000
Market value

Full Brand System

Name. Positioning. Visual identity direction. Voice. Sub-brand architecture — how garden storytelling, retreat curation, and the membership network live under one coherent world without collapsing into each other. The umbrella that protects all living brand creation. Strong guidelines. Trained voice GPTs. All brand identity pieces created and organized.

04
Month 4–5
$5,000–$8,000
Market value

Webflow Landing Page + Waitlist → CRM

A single-page website built to collect the right leads before the thing is fully built. Application-style intake — not open signups. Waitlist feeds directly into a CRM pipeline. Stripe-ready for deposit capture when the time comes. Built on Webflow for full flexibility.

05
Month 6
$3,000–$10,000
Market value

Investor Pitch Deck

Proof, promise, plan — in that order. Market data, existing assets, and case study proof first. The ecosystem vision second. The phased build, revenue model, and ask third. Designed to work for both capital investors and land partnership conversations.

Total market value $21,500–$51,000 → $9,000
03 Investment

Two phases.
One arc.

Root Intensive anchors the full build with handoff. Root Extended continues the work, keeps FMM as a partner, and helps it come to fruition. Root Extended is not required — the full build is complete in six months.

04 Questions That Need Answers

Still open.
Determined in month one.

These aren't obstacles — they're determined in month one.

→

What does the umbrella brand get named? The name is the first discovery.

→

Which idea becomes the MVP? The garden storytelling is the lowest-lift, highest-visibility proof of concept. We confirm that together in week one.

→

Which CRM tools fit the way you want to operate? We'll spec the right stack in month one based on the vision — not a template answer.

→

Capital investment or land partnerships — or both? The pitch deck works for either. We sharpen the target once we know who's in the room.

The ideas are already flowing. We need to build the architecture that holds it upright long enough for other people to see it too.

Start now.

6 months. 5 deliverables. 1 ecosystem.
